revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:成功 (任官)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:成功 (任官)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:成功 (任官)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:成功 (任官)]]
{{Referencing|date=14 May 2012 (Mon) 13:03 (UTC)}} '''Jōgō''' refers to a system under the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|qid=Q1134237|lt=Japanese Imperial Court|1=Royal court}} whereby candidates aspiring for {{ill|official positions|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official positions|1=East Asian government position|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452125}} would bear costs that were originally public expenses. These costs included expenditures for public works, maintenance of palace buildings, and repair of {{ill|temples and shrines|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temples and shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Temples and Shrines|qq=}}. In exchange, these candidates received appointments to {{ill|government positions|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government positions|1=East Asian government position|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452125}}. This was a form of {{ill|Venal office|en|Venal office|qid=Q255077|lt=office selling|1=Venal office}}.
== Overview == === Origins of Jōgō === The system of Jōgō developed after the {{ill|Regency period|ja|摂闘政治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regency period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, though its foundations trace back to the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō system|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 律令制|1=Ritsuryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q742460}}.
Under the {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|qid=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryō|1=Traditional Chinese law}} codes, individuals with a {{ill|Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Court Ranks in Japan|qid=Q99196082}} but no official title were assigned to the {{ill|San'iryō|ja|散位家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'iryō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Those with military decorations ({{ill|kun'i|ja|勤位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kun'i|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) served in the {{ill|Corps|en|Corps|qid=Q188497|lt=gundan|1=Corps}} (military units). However, as positions became limited, individuals were allowed to pay money instead of fulfilling their labor duties, a practice referred to as ''{{ill|Zokurōsen|ja|続労銭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokurōsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 贖労銭|1=Q11635147|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635147}}''. Furthermore, during the mid-{{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, individuals who contributed personal wealth to the construction of {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|qid=Q1207757|lt=provincial temples|1=Provincial temple}} or the {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|qid=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji|1=Tōdai-ji}} were granted court Ranks, a system known as ''{{ill|Kenbutsu Jōi|ja|献物叟位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenbutsu Jōi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. These precedents laid the foundation for the later Jōgō system.
Jōgō is broadly categorized into '''Juryō Jōgō''' ({{ill|Juryō Jōgō|ja|受領成功|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Juryō Jōgō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and '''Chige Jōgō''' ({{ill|Chige Jōgō|ja|地下成功|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chige Jōgō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). The former was utilized by provincial governors ({{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|qid=Q1351234|lt=kokushi|1=Kokushi (official)}}) to fund large-scale projects and events, seeking either reappointment or transfer. The latter was a smaller-scale system used by lower-Ranking {{ill|Chige officials|ja|地下官人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chige officials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 地下人|1=jige officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11424047}} for acquiring positions, primarily to a11ress shortages in funds or operating costs.
Notably, Jōgō aimed to secure positions rather than {{ill|Ranks|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ranks|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Court Ranks in Japan|qid=Q99196082}}. Although individuals might occasionally gain Ranks as a side benefit, the system’s primary purpose was appointing officials.
=== Chige Officials and Jōgō === Chige Jōgō evolved from ''{{ill|Zokurōsen|ja|続労銭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokurōsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 贖労銭|1=Q11635147|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635147}}'' and ''{{ill|Kenbutsu Jōi|ja|献物叟位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenbutsu Jōi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''. By the late {{ill|10th century|ja|十世紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10th century|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this system had been refined and formalized. It involved government bodies ({{ill|kanshi|ja|官司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kanshi|1=kanshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452091}}, temples, or event offices) applying to the court for authority to appoint candidates to {{ill|official posts|ja|官職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official posts|1=East Asian government position|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452125}} or confer honorary Ranks ({{ill|jōkyaku|ja|栄爵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jōkyaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 叙爵|1=Honorary Ranks|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11410718}}). Successful applicants paid fees ({{ill|jōryō|ja|任料|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jōryō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) to the requesting institution.
The process included: 1. Application for Jōgō permission ({{ill|Senji|ja|宣旨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senji|qq=}}). 2. Recruitment of candidates by the institution. 3. Collection of fees. 4. Submission of candidate lists to the court during the {{ill|Jomei|ja|除目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jomei|1=jimoku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11658210}} or {{ill|Joi|ja|叟位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceremonies. 5. Appointment based on these submissions.
=== Juryō Officials and Jōgō === Juryō Jōgō developed during the late {{ill|10th century|ja|十世紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10th century|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the court struggled with fiscal deficits. The system allowed governors to fund projects using their private wealth, in exchange for reappointment or transfer. The earliest recorded example is from {{ill|989 CE|ja|永宴三年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/989 CE|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} when {{ill|Fujiwara no Teishun|ja|藤原賞順|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Teishun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Minamoto no Norikata|ja|源乗方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Norikata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were appointed as {{ill|Tanba Province|ja|丹泥国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Governor and {{ill|Echizen Province|ja|赤前国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echizen Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Governor respectively, based on contributions to shrine and temple constructions ({{ill|Kamo shrines|ja|上下江社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shikibu-shō|en|Shikibu-shō|qid=Q3727885|lt=Ministry of Ceremonial Affairs|1=Shikibu-shō}}).
During the {{ill|Cloistered rule|en|Cloistered rule|qid=Q865416|lt=Insei|1=Cloistered rule}} period ({{ill|11th century|ja|十一世紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/11th century|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} onwards), Jōgō expanded significantly due to increased construction of {{ill|Imperial residences|ja|院居|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial residences|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and temples.
=== Decline and Abolition === With the establishment of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉導府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Jōgō began to wane. Policies like {{ill|Goseibai Shikimoku|ja|官成規范|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goseibai Shikimoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} discouraged unregulated appointments and emphasized Shogunate control over nominations. By the mid-{{ill|14th century|ja|十四世紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/14th century|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|室町導府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Jōgō ceased as local administrative systems evolved.
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Shijaku|ja|氏爵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijaku|1=Q11547816|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11547816}} * {{ill|Venal office|en|Venal office|qid=Q255077|lt=Office selling|1=Venal office}} * {{ill|Tax remission systems|ja|貢労銭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tax remission systems|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ritsuryō system reforms|ja|律令制度改革|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō system reforms|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Military aristocracy in Japan|ja|武家経濟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Military aristocracy in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
